explanation.summary: Proteins are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in A tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in A tier with an overall score of 60 from a base nutrition score of 57. Context lifted it by 3 points.

topPros.title: one of the best budget staple foods
topPros.explanation: It is unusually cheap, filling, and useful in real meals compared with a lot of healthy-food staples.

topCons.title: contains some anti-nutrients
topCons.explanation: Phytates may reduce mineral uptake somewhat.

topCons.title: can be rough on some people digestively
topCons.explanation: Not everyone handles legumes equally well.

topCons.title: needs some cooking effort
topCons.explanation: A small practical friction versus more convenient foods.

narrationNotes: Context improved the final tier case by 3 points.

closing.summary: Lentils has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

scenes.narrationText: 1.1g of fat. saturated fat, omega3, and polyunsaturated fat are doing most of the work. for a legume, this is exactly the kind of profile you want.
scenes.subtitleText: 1.1g of fat. saturated fat, omega3, and polyunsaturated fat are doing most of the work. for a legume, this is exactly the kind of profile you want.

scenes.narrationText: 60.1g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for legumes, the carbs look much better when fibre and protein are both backing them up.

scenes.narrationText: 25.8g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein support is a big part of what makes legumes worth it.

scenes.narrationText: on the plus side: one of the best budget staple foods because it is unusually cheap, filling, and useful in real meals compared with a lot of healthy-food staples. very filling for the calories because the fibre and protein combo gives it strong practical fullness. usually affordable and practical because makes it easy to use as a real staple rather than a niche health food.
